clusters.summary: [Daily Maverick] Former SA Air Force brigadier general Portia 'Posh' Anyamba, who has been sentenced to jail in the United States for being a foreign agent for South Africa, was apparently paid to go to events and secretly report back to officials from this country. clusters.headline: Uganda: Teacher Killed, Dozens of Students Injured in Train-Bus Collision At Mukono Railway Crossing
clusters.summary: [Nile Post] MUKONO: One teacher was killed and several students injured on Friday morning after a passenger train collided with a bus carrying Mwebaza High School students on a study trip to Jinja at the Namumira railway crossing in Mukono Municipality.

clusters.headline: Johor state polls: Political heavyweights take centre stage as parties chase undecided voters
clusters.summary: Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim, Deputy Prime Minister Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Parti Islam Se-Malaysia (PAS) president Abdul Hadi Awang have all been campaigning for their respective coalitions in a final push for votes.

clusters.headline: Democrat Graham Platner suspends campaign for key US Senate race after assault allegation
clusters.summary: The decision came days after a woman accused him of sexual assault, an accusation he says is "categorically false".

clusters.headline: Changi Airport opens new building with aerobridges for flights parked away from terminals
clusters.summary: The air-conditioned facility, complete with solar panels and a wheelchair-friendly ramp, will be tested on selected flights from August.

clusters.headline: South Sudan: Fifteen Years of Independence for South Sudan, but Still Little to Celebrate
clusters.summary: [RFI] South Sudan, the world's youngest country, on Thursday marks the 15th anniversary of its independence, following decades of war with Sudan, a 2005 peace agreement and a 2011 self-determination referendum in which almost 99 percent of voters backed statehood. clusters.headline: Graham Platner ends Senate bid. And, why Nolan Wells' death captured national attention
clusters.summary: Democrat Graham Platner ended his bid last night for U.S. Senate. And, prominent civil rights attorney Ben Crump has been enlisted to help get answers in the July Fourth weekend death of Nolan Wells.
